page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor operating at 2.5 GHz, with 8 physical cores and 1.0 thread per core. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include any local storage or GPU accelerators, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the ecs.sn2.xlarge sits primarily in the lower performance tiers. Its single-core and multi-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng, fall into the bottom 25% tier, with scores of 1105.29 and 5834.87 respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 324.59 GB/sec (bottom 25%) and a write large block speed of 33.29 GB/sec (middle 50%). Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression score in the bottom 10%, while multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is in the bottom 25%. Geekbench and Passmark scores similarly place the instance in the weak to average categories, with a Passmark CPU score of 6995.67 (middle 50%) and a Passmark memory score of 1670.71 (bottom 25%).

The primary tradeoff of the ecs.sn2.xlarge is its modest processing speed and lack of local storage, which limits its utility for high-throughput database operations or disk-heavy tasks. However, its dedicated CPU allocation and balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio offer stable, predictable resource density for workloads that do not require high-performance computing. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for moderate, non-burstable workloads. Suitable workloads include basic web serving, small-scale LLM inference for small models (such as the 135M model, which achieves average text generation speeds of 99.85 tokens/sec), and general-purpose application hosting.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor at 2.5 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates modest performance, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU metrics falling into the bottom 25% tier. While memory write speeds and Passmark CPU scores reach average mid-tier levels, single-threaded compression and text processing benchmarks remain in the bottom 10%. Given its dedicated CPU allocation and balanced 1:4 memory-to-core ratio, the ecs.sn2.xlarge is suited for moderate general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, and lightweight model inference where high-speed local storage is not required.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2.xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth, suited for moderate workloads and basic web serving.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2.xlarge features 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, delivering stable general-purpose performance.
tagline: A dedicated general-purpose instance featuring eight Intel Xeon cores and balanced memory for stable, entry-level enterprise workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory (4.0 GB per core)
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for basic web serving and lightweight model inference
